name: Hints for Using a Candy Thermometer
recipeInstructions:
Learn all the secrets of really GREAT candy making!
1. Before starting, test your thermometer by immersing it in cold water and bringing the water to a boil at sea level (212°F). If the boiling point is 210°F, reduce cooking time by 2 degrees. At most places, water boils around 208°F.
2. It's best to make candy on sunny days when atmospheric pressure is higher.
3. I recommend using a Taylor or Wilmont thermometer.
4. For candies that must remain in the pan for cooling, use a thin, good quality pan. If you leave the thermometer in the pan after removing the candy from the stove, the temperature may rise slightly even after removal.
5. Use a heavy pan for fudges, fondants, and other items that are dumped immediately.
